title: Standard proof envelope with IANA-registerable content type

artifacts.description: IANA media type registration template for application/bolyra-proof+cbor, CBOR schema definition (CDDL), field semantics, versioning strategy, and interop requirements

ProofEnvelope TypeScript class with encode()/decode() methods using cbor-x, typed fields for proofBytes, publicSignals, circuitId, provingSystem (groth16|plonk), version, and optional delegationChain array
Add ProofEnvelope, ProvingSystem, and DelegationChainEntry type exports to the public API surface
ProofEnvelope Python dataclass with encode()/decode() methods using the cbor2 library, mirroring the TS schema exactly
Re-export ProofEnvelope from the top-level package
Round-trip encode/decode tests, cross-version compatibility tests, malformed input rejection tests, and size benchmark against raw JSON
Python round-trip tests plus cross-language interop test: encode in TS (via subprocess), decode in Python, and vice versa
Developer guide: wire format diagram, field reference table, content-type negotiation example, migration guide for existing LangChain/CrewAI/MCP integrations
Formal CDDL (Concise Data Definition Language) schema for the CBOR envelope, machine-checkable and suitable for inclusion in IANA registration

steps: Define CBOR schema in CDDL: fields are version (uint), circuitId (tstr), provingSystem (tstr enum: groth16|plonk), proofBytes (bstr), publicSignals (array of bstr), delegationChain (optional array of bstr). Pin field indices so future additions are append-only.
Add cbor-x as a dependency in sdk/package.json and cbor2 in sdk-python/pyproject.toml; verify license compatibility (MIT/Apache) before committing.
Implement ProofEnvelope class in sdk/src/proof-envelope.ts with static encode(fields): Uint8Array and decode(bytes): ProofEnvelope; throw typed BolyraError on schema violations.
Add ProofEnvelope and related types to sdk/src/index.ts public exports; bump sdk version to 0.3.0 in package.json.
Implement ProofEnvelope dataclass in sdk-python/bolyra/proof_envelope.py with encode() -> bytes and decode(data: bytes) -> ProofEnvelope using cbor2; raise BolyraError on decode failure.
Write TS unit tests covering: round-trip identity, missing required field rejection, unknown future field tolerance (forward-compat), and byte-size vs JSON baseline.
Write Python unit tests covering the same cases plus a cross-language interop test that shells out to node -e to encode a fixture in TS then decodes it in Python.
Update LangChain integration (integrations/langchain/) to use ProofEnvelope.encode/decode instead of ad-hoc JSON serialization.
Update MCP integration (integrations/mcp/ and examples/mcp-demo/) to attach Content-Type: application/bolyra-proof+cbor header when transmitting envelopes over HTTP.
Write spec/proof-envelope-content-type.md following RFC 6838 template sections: media type name, required parameters, encoding considerations, security considerations, IANA registration contact.
Add spec/proof-envelope.cddl as the normative CDDL schema referenced from both the spec doc and the SDK source as a comment header.
Run full test suite (npm test + pytest) and confirm no regressions; add a size-regression check that rejects encode output > 2x the raw proof bytes.
Update CHANGELOG.md and sdk/QUICKSTART.md with ProofEnvelope usage examples.
dependencies: cbor-x (npm) — CBOR encode/decode for TypeScript SDK
cbor2 (PyPI) — CBOR encode/decode for Python SDK
RFC 6838 — Media type specifications and registration procedures
Existing BolyraError typed error infrastructure in sdk/src/errors.ts
Existing AgentCredential and HumanIdentity types for publicSignals shape reference
Delegation circuit output format (delegationChain array element schema)
